channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Sinner and Zverev Reach Wimbledon Final in Dominant Showings
item.description: Jannik Sinner and Alexander Zverev made a clean sweep to the Wimbledon final after crushing their opponents. Sinner ended Novak Djokovic's bid for a record 25th Grand Slam with a 6-4, 6-4, 6-4 win, wh...

item.title: Study Suggests Mount Etna May Have Formed Through a Rare Magma Transport Mechanism
item.description: Researchers from the University of Lausanne have proposed a new explanation for the origin of Mount Etna, Europe's most active volcano, challenging the traditional models used to explain how large vol...

item.title: Seth Rogen and Olivia Wilde's On-Screen Chemistry in 'The Invite'
item.description: Seth Rogen and Olivia Wilde have found a unique dynamic in their on-screen roles, particularly in their latest film 'The Invite'. The article explores their collaboration, highlighting how their chemi...

item.title: Municipal Financial Crisis Threatens South Africa's Economy
item.description: A new Bureau for Economic Research (BER) report highlights the severe financial strain on South Africa's municipalities, warning of systemic failure risks if current trends continue. The report, relea...

item.title: Delhi to mandate water meters for borewells to regulate groundwater usage and billing
item.description: The Delhi government is drafting a policy requiring all domestic and commercial borewells to be fitted with water meters, linking water bills to actual groundwater extraction. This move aims to monito...

item.title: Frog-derived bacterium shows promise in eliminating cancer tumors in mice
item.description: Scientists at the Japan Advanced Institute of Science and Technology (JAIST) discovered a naturally occurring bacterium, Ewingella americana, from Japanese tree frogs that completely eliminated colore...

item.title: Maine Senate Candidate Graham Platner Withdraws from Race After Sexual Assault Allegations
item.description: Graham Platner, the Democratic candidate for Maine's Senate race, has officially withdrawn from the contest after submitting the required paperwork to remove his name from the ballot. The decision com...

item.title: Dispute Over NSW DPP's Conduct Threatens Public Trust in Rape Prosecutions
item.description: A bitter dispute over the conduct of NSW's top prosecutor has exposed a deeper divide over how the state prosecutes rape cases, threatening public trust in the law. A highly contested parliamentary in...

item.title: Security Forces Conduct Joint Operations Against Terrorists in Quetta
item.description: Security forces, including the Pakistan Army, Frontier Corps (FC), and police, have intensified operations against terrorists in Quetta's Shaban area. A joint operation since July 5 has resulted in th...

item.title: Wildfires devastate Europe, claiming lives and leaving dozens missing in Spain and France
item.description: Wildfires have ravaged parts of Europe, with devastating consequences in Spain and France. In southern Spain, a wildfire near Almeria claimed at least 12 lives, including four British nationals, while...

item.title: SK Hynix's Nasdaq Listing Offers Canadian Investors New AI Investment Opportunities
item.description: SK Hynix, a South Korean semiconductor manufacturer, has listed on the Nasdaq, providing Canadian investors with a new avenue to participate in AI-driven hardware. The company's American Depositary Re...

item.title: Pilipinas Today Ad Network Spends ₱138M on Political Campaigns
item.description: A sprawling network of Facebook pages and ad funders has spent over ₱138 million on political and social issues campaigns in the Philippines, according to a detailed analysis by Rappler. The network, ...

item.title: Andy Burnham's Challenges as Prime Minister: Key Issues to Address
item.description: Andy Burnham, set to become the UK's next prime minister, inherits a complex array of challenges from his predecessor Keir Starmer. These include reforming welfare policies, particularly disability be...

item.title: Quetta protest concludes after kidnapped tribal men are released
item.description: A protest in Quetta, triggered by the killing of five tribesmen, injury of eight, and abduction of 11 from Hanna Urak Valley, concluded after the release of the kidnapped individuals. Negotiations bet...

item.title: Scientists explore Dyson spheres around red and white dwarfs as potential alien megastructures
item.description: A new study suggests that the coldest stars in the galaxy, particularly red dwarfs and white dwarfs, could be candidates for hosting alien megastructures like Dyson spheres. Researchers propose that t...

item.title: Senate panel approves bill to extend blue passport entitlement to dependent children of former lawmakers while reviewing key governance and accountability issues
item.description: A Senate Standing Committee on Interior and Narcotics Control has unanimously approved the Members of Parliament Salaries and Allowances (Amendment) Bill, 2026, which proposes extending the entitlemen...

item.title: Protecting Jewish Australians should not come at the expense of limiting legitimate criticism of Israel, opinion argues
item.description: In this opinion piece, former Canberra Times editor Jack Waterford argues that Jewish Australians deserve strong protection from violence, intimidation and discrimination, particularly following the r...

item.title: Cuba Faces Second Islandwide Blackout This Week Amid Grid Issues
item.description: Cuba is experiencing its second widespread power outage of the week, with the national electricity grid struggling to maintain stability. The blackout, which affects the entire island, has left reside...

item.title: Typhoon Inday and Habagat cause 17 deaths, 112,000 families affected
item.description: The Office of Civil Defense (OCD) confirmed 17 reported deaths caused by Typhoon Inday (international name: Bavi) and the enhanced Southwest Monsoon (Habagat). Deputy Administrator Bernardo Rafaelito ...

item.title: Yanga Appoints South African Coach with Extensive Football Experience
item.description: Young Africans Sports Club (Yanga) has appointed a new head coach with significant experience in South African football, aiming to strengthen its position as a leading East African club. The coach, pr...
